font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">exit</code>), use<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">cd /path/to/your/project</code><span>&nbsp;</span>in your terminal, and run the<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Initialize a new directory</strong>: Once in your new project folder, run<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">/init</code><span>&nbsp;</span>inside OpenCode to scan the codebase and create an<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">AGENTS.md</code><span>&nbsp;</span>file, which helps the agent understand that specific project's structure.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">The VS Code model is great for "browsing"—you open the tool first, then decide what to work on. It treats the editor like a blank canvas.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">OpenCode’s design is more<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">"context-first."</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Because it’s built around AI agents and codebase understanding, it wants to know<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">what</em><span>&nbsp;</span>it’s looking at the moment it starts. By launching from a specific directory, it can immediately index files and set up its "brain" for that specific project without you having to point to it manually.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Why it can feel clunky:</strong><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">It breaks the habit of using a "File &gt; Open" workflow.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">Switching projects requires a bit more friction (restarting or using commands).</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Why it’s powerful:</strong><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Zero setup:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>If you’re already in your terminal working on a feature, you don't have to navigate again inside a GUI.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Deep Context:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>The agent is "locked in" to your project boundaries from second one, reducing the chance of it hallucinating code from a different project.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Essentially, VS Code is a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">general-purpose editor</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>that you bring projects to, while OpenCode acts more like a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">specialized teammate</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>that you "summon" into your current workspace.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; Most developers agree that having to exit and re-enter a tool just to change folders feels like a step backward in UX</div>. While OpenCode's "context-first" approach is its biggest strength, it shouldn't come at the cost of basic navigation.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; The<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">/cd</code><span>&nbsp;</span>Command<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">There is a high demand for a simple<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">/cd &lt;path&gt;</code><span>&nbsp;</span>command within the TUI. This would allow you to:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; A Global<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">/session</code><span>&nbsp;</span>Browser<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Currently, the<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">/session</code><span>&nbsp;</span>dialog (accessible via<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Ctrl+P</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>or typing the command) is often scoped only to your current folder. While this doesn't help once you're<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">already</em><span>&nbsp;</span>inside, a more robust implementation would allow:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">New Windows</strong>: A command like<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">/new-window &lt;path&gt;</code><span>&nbsp;</span>that stays in the TUI but switches the project backend.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">The current design is a bit "locked in" because OpenCode relies heavily on<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">indexing</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>your files to provide accurate AI responses. Changing directories mid-session means the agent has to "wipe its brain" and re-index the new folder, which can be resource-heavy.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">When you install OpenCode via<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">pnpm</strong>, the data is split between the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">package files</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(managed by pnpm's content-addressable store) and your<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">user/project data</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(managed by OpenCode itself).<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">1. The OpenCode Binary &amp; Dependencies (pnpm Store)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Because pnpm uses a content-addressable storage system, the actual files for the OpenCode package are not stored multiple times. Instead:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Global Store</strong>: The core files are kept in a single location on your disk (e.g.,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">~/.local/share/pnpm/store</code><span>&nbsp;</span>on Linux or<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">~/Library/pnpm/store</code><span>&nbsp;</span>on macOS).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; overflow-x: auto;"><pre><code><span>{
  </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"permission"</span><span>: {
    </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"external_directory"</span><span>: {
      </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"~/projects/other-repo/**"</span><span>: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"allow"</span><span>,
      </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"/tmp/logs/**"</span><span>: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"allow"</span><span>

  }

position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Approval Prompt</strong>: Even with "allow" set, some versions or security levels may still trigger a one-time confirmation prompt when the agent first attempts to touch a path outside the workspace.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Tool Scope</strong>: This permission applies to almost all built-in tools, including<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">read</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">write</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">edit</code>, and<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">bash</code>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Path Expansion</strong>: Using<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">~</code><span>&nbsp;</span>or home expansion only works for how you write the path in the config; it does not automatically make your entire home directory accessible.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Absolute Paths Required</strong>: When instructing the agent to create a file outside its current folder, you should provide the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">absolute path</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>to ensure it doesn't get confused.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Be cautious when allowing access to sensitive areas like your<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">.ssh</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">.aws</code>, or root system folders. Giving the agent<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">bash</code><span>&nbsp;</span>access to an external directory essentially gives it the same permissions as your user account in that location.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">OpenCode is fundamentally a<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">headless agent</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>with a terminal user interface (TUI) built on top of it. While most users interact with it through its polished TUI, it is fully functional as a standard CLI for programmatic or direct command-line use.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">CLI Usage and Headless Operation<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">If you want to bypass the interactive TUI, the OpenCode CLI supports direct execution of tasks via flags and subcommands:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Direct Execution</strong>: Use<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">opencode run "Your prompt here"</code><span>&nbsp;</span>to execute a single task directly from your shell without entering the interactive interface.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Programmatic Control</strong>: It includes a client/server architecture where the backend runs a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">local REST API</strong>. This allows you to "drive" the agent from other tools, scripts, or even a mobile app while it executes tasks on your local files.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Automation</strong>: Commands like<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">OpenCode's CLI is highly proven as a<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">pluggable infrastructure</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>layer, meaning it is designed more as a "backend for your terminal" than just a standalone tool. While many users stick to the TUI, the underlying CLI and its server-side architecture have enabled several stable abstractions.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; Ecosystem<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Editor "Bridges"</strong>: The most common abstraction is<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">opencode.nvim</span>, which wraps the CLI to provide a sub-terminal and rich diff previews directly inside Neovim. Similar integrations exist for<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Zed</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">VS Code</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Model Context Protocol (MCP)</strong>: OpenCode acts as a host for<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">MCP servers</span>. This allows the CLI to gain "superpowers" (like database access or specialized API tools) using a standardized protocol, making it a proven orchestrator for external tools.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">GitHub Actions Integration</strong>: You can run OpenCode headlessly in CI/CD pipelines. It is proven for automated tasks like responding to issue comments, reviewing PRs, and creating branches based on repository context.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Custom Tooling &amp; Plugins</strong>: Developers build "skills" and custom tools that the CLI can execute. The<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">OpenCode Plugin Guide</span><span>&nbsp;</span>shows how to hook into event handlers and shell integration for specialized company workflows.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Programmatic Control</strong>: Commands like<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">opencode run "task"</code><span>&nbsp;</span>allow for non-interactive execution, where success is verified by external gates like tests or linters rather than just AI confidence.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Local REST API</strong>: When you run OpenCode, it starts a local JS HTTP server. This abstraction allows other apps or scripts to "drive" the agent via standard HTTP requests.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Type-Safe Client Code</strong>: OpenCode uses<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">Stainless</span><span>&nbsp;</span>to automatically generate high-quality SDK client code, allowing developers to build their own UIs or automation scripts on top of the CLI with full type safety.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; Set Environment Variables<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">OpenCode can recognize certain environment variables to authenticate with providers. For a standard OpenAI-compatible setup, you can export these in your terminal or add them to your shell profile (e.g.,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">~/.bashrc</code><span>&nbsp;</span>or<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">API Key:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">~/.config/opencode/opencode.json</code><span>&nbsp;</span>(global) or in your project's root as<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex;   </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"providers"</span><span>: {
    </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"my-custom-provider"</span><span>: {
      </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"type"</span><span>: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"openai"</span><span>,
      </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"baseURL"</span><span>: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"https://your-custom-url/v1"</span><span>,

        }
      }
    }
  }
}
</span></code></pre></div></div><div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px 4px 16px 16px; If OpenCode isn't picking up your<span>&nbsp;</span></div><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">~/.zshrc</code><span>&nbsp;</span>to force the mapping:<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 20px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Prefix your variables</strong>: OpenCode specifically looks for the<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">OPENCODE_</code><span>&nbsp;</span>prefix to map to its internal provider logic.</span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">export OPENCODE_OPENAI_API_KEY="your-api-key"</code></span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">export OPENCODE_OPENAI_BASE_URL="https://your-custom-url/v1"</code></span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Specify the model at launch</strong>: Even with the keys set, OpenCode often defaults to Anthropic/Claude. You have to tell it to use the OpenAI provider you just "configured" in your env:</span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">Run:<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">opencode --model openai/your-model-id</code></span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Refresh your shell</strong>: This is the most common "stuck" point. Run<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">source ~/.zshrc</code><span>&nbsp;</span>in every open terminal tab, or the new variables won't exist in that session.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; Some versions may also look for<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">OPENCODE_OPENAI_APIKEY</code><span>&nbsp;</span>(no underscore before KEY).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Base URL Mapping</strong>: If you are using a custom base URL, you<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">must</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>also set<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">OPENCODE_OPENAI_BASE_URL</code>. Without this, OpenCode will send your key to the default<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">api.openai.com</code><span>&nbsp;</span>endpoint.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Config Precedence</strong>: OpenCode prioritizes configuration files over environment variables. font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">~/.config/opencode/</code>, it will ignore your<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">_API_KEY</code>, some internal schemas look for<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">OPENCODE_OPENAI_APIKEY</code><span>&nbsp;</span>(no underscore before KEY). Try setting both to be safe.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Config Precedence</strong>: OpenCode follows a strict hierarchy.<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">A local<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">opencode.json</code><span>&nbsp;</span>in your current directory will override your environment variables</strong>.</span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">Check for:<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">./opencode.json</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">./.opencode/opencode.json</code>, or<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">~/.config/opencode/opencode.json</code>.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Model Prefix</strong>: Even with the keys set, you must tell OpenCode to<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">use</em><span>&nbsp;</span>the OpenAI provider. If you just run<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">opencode</code>, it may default to Anthropic.</span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Fix</strong>: Launch with<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">opencode --model openai/&lt;your-model-name&gt;</code>. This forces it to look for the<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">OPENCODE_OPENAI_</code><span>&nbsp;</span>environment variables.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Zsh Sourcing</strong>: Ensure you have run<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">source ~/.zshrc</code><span>&nbsp;</span>in your active terminal. Variables exported in<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">.zshrc</code><span>&nbsp;</span>are not automatically pushed to already-open terminal sessions.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; If this works, the issue is your<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">.zshrc</code><span>&nbsp;</span>sourcing or a hidden config file:<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div style="margin-block: 24px 32px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--jINu6c); font-weight: 400;"><div><div style="background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 16px 16px 4px 4px; overflow: auto hidden; padding: 12px 16px 0px;"><div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 26px; letter-spacing: 0px;">bash</div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; overflow-x: auto;"><pre><code><span>OPENCODE_OPENAI_API_KEY=</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"your_key"</span><span> OPENCODE_OPENAI_BASE_URL=</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"your_url"</span><span> opencode --model openai/your-model
